title: F046 — Recovery Spiral

requiredObjects: central registry or shared protocol layer
central symbolic heart or integration point

composition.camera: three-quarter orthographic or centered symbolic perspective
composition.layout: central topology with clean surrounding annotations
composition.colorLogic: spectrum or luminous gradient only when it clarifies geometry
composition.labelPolicy: labels optional; prefer clean geometry over text-heavy design

qaChecklist: Avoids forbidden objects and fake text
Does not overuse mystical or decorative elements

prompts.providerAgnostic:
Create a publication-quality scientific book figure titled "F046 — Recovery Spiral".
Purpose: Recovery is represented as a spiral rather than a straight line, allowing return, repetition, and growth.

Manuscript context: Moral inventory is a practical form of recursive observation. The person does not merely feel resentment. The person observes resentment. The person asks what fear, wound, expectation, attachment, or false story is embedded inside it. This is not self-condemnation. It is pattern recognition. In William James' language, religious experience often involves conversion, surrender, and reorganization of the self around a deeper center james1902Varieties. In Viktor Frankl's language, meaning can become a path through suffering rather than an escape from it frankl1946Meaning. FHQCM translates this into process terms: observation changes recursion. What is seen clearly can begin to be reintegrated. Recovery as Spiral Recovery rarely moves as a straight line. It spirals. A person returns to the same wound, but from a different level of awareness. The same resentment appears, but now with more honesty. The same fear appears, but now with more support. The same grief appears, but now with more capacity. F046 — Recovery SpiralRecovery is represented as a spiral rather than a straight line, allowing return, repetition, and growth.F046-recovery-spiral This is why relapse language must be handled carefully. A return to an old pattern does not always mean nothing has changed. It may reveal where the pattern still needs integration. The spiral remembers. Somatic Coherence Emotional sobriety is not only cognitive. It is bodily. Breath, sleep, food, movement, prayer, sweat, tears, community, and safe touch all participate in regulation. The FHQCM therefore treats coherence as embodied integration rather than mere intellectual insight. A person may understand a pattern long before the nervous system believes it is safe to release the pattern. Red Road and Communal Repair Recovery traditions often understand what modern individualism forgets: healing is communal. The person heals in relation to land, ancestors, Creator, community, body, story, and responsibility.

Scientific/conceptual concepts: observer participation, topological recursion, holographic/fractal structure, federated systems architecture, symbolic integration.
Required visual objects: observer axis or observation node, information flow arrows, toroidal geometry, nested or recursive field lines, nodes, links, central registry or shared protocol layer, central symbolic heart or integration point, spiral or healing path.
Recommended short labels: observer, information, recursion, field, node, federation.

Composition: central topology with clean surrounding annotations.
Camera/view: three-quarter orthographic or centered symbolic perspective.
Visual hierarchy: primary manifold or geometry → recursive field lines → observer or axis if relevant → caption-level meaning implied by structure.
Color logic: spectrum or luminous gradient only when it clarifies geometry.
Label policy: labels optional; prefer clean geometry over text-heavy design.

Avoid: fake citations, fake equations, unreadable text, watermarks, copyrighted characters, random mystical clutter, misleading scientific labels, random galaxies unless explicitly required, decorative clutter.

Make it clean, serious, elegant, uncluttered, and suitable for an academic manuscript.
